pi_items.source: OpenBCI official documentation (https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/) states 'By default, all GUI recordings are stored in User/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings on all operating systems'. On Linux with user 'ga', this resolves to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings. Confirmed in setup_openbci.sh line 16.

OpenBCI GUI Widget documentation (https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/) specifies default marker keyboard shortcuts: Z=1, X=2, C=3, V=4, and Shift combinations for 5-8. Markers 1, 2, 3 are standard and directly correspond to default UI buttons.

Task requirement for temporal separation of markers. At standard OpenBCI Cyton sample rate of 250 Hz, 100 samples = 0.4 seconds. export_result.sh line 165 checks: 'if (last_line - first_line) > 100' to validate separation. Task description specifies 'Wait about 2 seconds between each marker', which at 250 Hz would be 500 samples—well exceeding the 100-sample minimum threshold.

Reasonable minimum for OpenBCI recording with markers. A 10 KB file at typical CSV row size (~115 bytes/row including header) represents ~89 rows = ~0.36 seconds of data at 250 Hz. To accommodate 3 markers with 100-sample separation requires ~300 samples (~1.2 seconds) = ~34.5 KB. Thus 10 KB is a reasonable but lenient minimum threshold. Verifier checks: file_size > 10000 bytes (line 54).

privileged_info_summary: This task uses OpenBCI GUI's SYNTHETIC (algorithmic) mode to generate synthetic EEG data and record event markers. The recording directory is /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings. The task requires insertion of markers with values 1, 2, and 3—standard default keyboard shortcuts (Z, X, C) in OpenBCI's Marker Mode widget. These markers must be temporally separated by at least 100 samples (0.4 seconds at the standard 250 Hz Cyton sampling rate). The minimum file size threshold of 10 KB is a reasonable lower bound for a valid recording, though proper execution with 2-second separation between markers would produce ~34.5 KB of data.
